title: docker build not working with podman.service
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**BUG REPORT**

/kind bug


**Description**

Docker build is not working for me with podman socket.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create Dockerfile
`FROM docker.io/library/alpine:latest`

2.DOCKER_API_VERSION=1.40 DOCKER_HOST=unix:////run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock docker build .


**Describe the results you received:**
```
Sending build context to Docker daemon  3.072kB
request returned Bad Request for API route and version http://%2F%2Frun%2Fuser%2F1000%2Fpodman%2Fpodman.sock/v1.40/build?buildargs=%7B%7D&cachefrom=%5B%5D&cgroupparent=&cpuperiod=0&cpuquota=0&cpusetcpus=&cpusetmems=&cpushares=0&dockerfile=Dockerfile&labels=%7B%7D&memory=0&memswap=0&networkmode=default&rm=1&shmsize=0&target=&ulimits=null&version=1, check if the server supports the requested API version
```
Output from systemctl --user status podman.service:
```
Sep 24 13:26:43 nbitc01 podman[1023418]: @ - - [24/Sep/2022:13:26:43 +0200] "HEAD /_ping HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "" "Docker-Client/20.10.18 (linux)"
Sep 24 13:26:43 nbitc01 podman[1023418]: time="2022-09-24T13:26:43+02:00" level=info msg="Request Failed(Bad Request): failed to parse query parameter 'cacheFrom': \"[]\": invalid repo \"[]\": must contain registry and repository: invalid reference format"
Sep 24 13:26:43 nbitc01 podman[1023418]: @ - - [24/Sep/2022:13:26:43 +0200] "POST /v1.40/build?buildargs=%7B%7D&cachefrom=%5B%5D&cgroupparent=&cpuperiod=0&cpuquota=0&cpusetcpus=&cpusetmems=&cpushares=0&dockerfile=Dockerfile&labels=%7B%7D&memory=0&memswap=0&networkmode=default&rm=1&shmsize=0&target=&ulimits=null&version=1 HTTP/1.1" 400 0 "" "Docker-Client/20.10.18 (linux)"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should build.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
`podman build .` works as expected..
Also docker compose (v2) works fine

1. sudo dnf install docker-compose-plugin
2. export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0
3. docker-compose.yml
```
version: "3.9"
services:
  test:
    build: .
```
4. docker compose build
5. success
```docker compose build --no-cache
Sending build context to Docker daemon     216B
STEP 1/1: FROM docker.io/library/alpine:latest
COMMIT docker.io/library/docker-test-test:latest
--> 9c6f0724472
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/docker-test-test:latest
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/alpine:latest
9c6f0724472873bb50a2ae67a9e7adcb57673a183cea8b06eb778dca859181b5
Successfully built 9c6f07244728
Successfully tagged docker-test-test
```

That's docker compose, not docker-compose, so the v2 plugin for docker...


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 16:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I wonder why did `docker client` set incorrect `cacheFrom` or why did it set it at all ? Is buildkit enabled on your client ? This request payload does not looks correct as per the documentation https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.41/#tag/Image/operation/ImageBuild

Could you try again disabling the buildkit ?

> I wonder why did `docker client` set incorrect `cacheFrom` or why did it set it at all ? Is buildkit enabled on your client ? This request payload does not looks correct as per the documentation https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.41/#tag/Image/operation/ImageBuild
> 
> Could you try again disabling the buildkit ?

Is it really incorrect? Docu says it should be a json array. It's type is "string" so it's a json array encoded/escaped as string.

Is it double-escaped or are those extra backslashes related to logging?

By the way, having a value in that array does not work either (through --cache-from).

I think following line broke compatibility https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go#L404 for cases where `cachefrom=[]` is set in payload , it should first parse and ignore if its empty array instead of directly trying to create a named reference.

I'll open a PR for this.
Hi @flouthoc I'd be interested if you already created a PR for this?
Hi @DenisaIoanaMatei , I have not created any PR for this till now, feel free to take is issue if you are interested in fixing this :) . Thanks

Hi,

@flouthoc I have found this issue while trying podman 4.3 with a docker client, and got a similar issue. I fear that any "docker build" when using the real docker client and the podman socket is broken with release 4.3.

I don't understand this part of the code you point: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go#L404 but here you seem to consider that "cacheFrom" is just a simple string, naming a single repo. And it looks like docker sends an array of repo, not just one. So the problem is not ignoring the empty array, but to really parse "cacheFrom" as an array (and maybe also "cacheTo" similarly ?). Or am I wrong ?

Cheers,
Romain

@Romain-Geissler-1A Yes you are correct we have to refactor it to accept array. @DenisaIoanaMatei are you still working on this issue ? If you are short on time I can just take it and push it to completion.
Hi @flouthoc I was working on it but only a bit so would be more than happy if you take over and push it to completion 🙏

@Romain-Geissler-1A buildah's distributed cache works on repos not on images so its fundamentally different from docker's remote cache and more like `kaniko`'s distributed cache, but sure it can accept array of repo's in future and that must be fixed at buildah end.

As of now issue is that docker sets `cacheFrom` for every build command even if the option is not used at all, following PR should unlock it https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16380 and we can process whole array when we add this feature to buildah.
